steps.title: Use substitution for the repeated expression
steps.content:
The term $(3-7x)$ appears on both sides of the equation. Let $u = 3-7x$ to simplify. The equation becomes:
$$16 - 5u = 4 - 6u$$

steps.title: Solve for u
steps.content:
Collect $u$ terms on the left and constants on the right:
$$16 - 5u + 6u = 4$$
$$16 + u = 4$$
$$u = -12$$

Since $u = 3-7x$, the value of $3-7x$ is $-12$.
